memberdef.definition: typedef void(* flash_area_cb_t) (const struct flash_area *fa, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: flash_area_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Flash map iteration callback.
Retrieve partitions flash area from the flash_map.

detaileddescription.para: Function Retrieves flash_area from flash_map for given partition.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer which has to reference flash_area. If ID is unknown, it will be NULL on output.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -EACCES if the flash_map is not available , -ENOENT if ID is unknown, -ENODEV if there is no driver attached to the area.

briefdescription.para: Close flash_area.

detaileddescription.para: Reserved for future usage and external projects compatibility reason. Currently is NOP.

briefdescription.para: Verify that a device assigned to flash area is ready for use.

detaileddescription.para: Indicates whether the provided flash area has a device known to be in a state where it can be used with Flash Map API.
This can be used with struct flash_area pointers captured from PARTITION(). At minimum this means that the device has been successfully initialized.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to flash_area object to check.
If the device is ready for use.
If the device is not ready for use or if a NULL pointer is passed as flash area pointer or device pointer within flash area object is NULL.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct flash_area *fa, off_t off, void *dst, size_t len)
memberdef.name: flash_area_read

briefdescription.para: Read flash area data.

detaileddescription.para: Read data from flash area. Area readout boundaries are asserted before read request. API has the same limitation regard read-block alignment and size as wrapped flash driver.

parameterdescription.para: Offset relative from beginning of flash area to read

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct flash_area *fa, off_t off, const void *src, size_t len)
memberdef.name: flash_area_write

briefdescription.para: Write data to flash area.

detaileddescription.para: Write data to flash area. Area write boundaries are asserted before write request. API has the same limitation regard write-block alignment and size as wrapped flash driver.

parameterdescription.para: Offset relative from beginning of flash area to write
Buffer with data to be written

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct flash_area *src_fa, off_t src_off, const struct flash_area *dst_fa, off_t dst_off, off_t len, uint8_t *buf, size_t buf_size)
memberdef.name: flash_area_copy

briefdescription.para: Copy flash memory from one flash area to another.

detaileddescription.para: Copy data to flash area. Area boundaries are asserted before copy request.

parameterdescription.para: Offset relative from beginning of source flash area.
Offset relative from beginning of destination flash area.
Number of bytes to copy, in bytes.
Pointer to a buffer of size buf_size.
Size of the buffer pointed to by buf.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct flash_area *fa, off_t off, size_t len)
memberdef.name: flash_area_erase

briefdescription.para: Erase flash area.

detaileddescription.para: Erase given flash area range. Area boundaries are asserted before erase request. API has the same limitation regard erase-block alignment and size as wrapped flash driver.

parameterdescription.para: Offset relative from beginning of flash area.
Number of bytes to be erase

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct flash_area *fa, off_t off, size_t len)
memberdef.name: flash_area_flatten

briefdescription.para: Erase flash area or fill with erase-value.

detaileddescription.para: On program-erase devices this function behaves exactly like flash_area_erase. On RAM non-volatile device it will call erase, if driver provides such callback, or will fill given range with erase-value defined by driver. This function should be only used by code that has not been written to directly support devices that do not require erase and rely on device being erased prior to some operations. Note that emulated erase, on devices that do not require, is done via write, which affects endurance of device.

parameterdescription.para: Offset relative from beginning of flash area.
Number of bytes to be erase

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

briefdescription.para: Get write block size of the flash area.

detaileddescription.para: Currently write block size might be treated as read block size, although most of drivers supports unaligned readout.

simplesect.para: Alignment restriction for flash writes in [B].

briefdescription.para: Retrieve info about sectors within the area.

parameterdescription.para: On input Capacity of sectors, on output number of sectors Retrieved.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail. Especially returns -ENOMEM if There are too many flash pages on the flash_area to fit in the array.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve info about sectors within the area.

parameterdescription.para: On input Capacity of sectors, on output number of sectors retrieved.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail. Especially returns -ENOMEM if There are too many flash pages on the flash_area to fit in the array.

briefdescription.para: Iterate over flash map.
Check whether given flash area has supporting flash driver in the system.

simplesect.para: 1 On success. -ENODEV if no driver match.

briefdescription.para: Get driver for given flash area.

simplesect.para: device driver.

briefdescription.para: Get the value expected to be read when accessing any erased flash byte.

detaileddescription.para: This API is compatible with the MCUBoot's porting layer.

simplesect.para: Byte value of erase memory.

briefdescription.para: Returns non-0 value if partition of given DTS node label exists.

simplesect.para: non-0 if partition node exists and is enabled; 0 if node does not exist, is not enabled or is not partition.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_EXISTS(), this cannot use __DEPRECATED_MACRO as it causes usage of the macro to fail with compiler errors.
Get flash area ID from partition DTS node label.

parameterdescription.para: DTS node label of a partition

simplesect.para: flash area ID

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_ID()
Get partition offset from DTS node label.

detaileddescription.para: Note: This only works from a top level fixed-partitions node, top level fixed-subpartitions node or fixed-partitions node inside of 1 layer of a fixed-subpartitions node, it will not work for multiple layers of fixed-subpartitions nodes, though this works on all instances of zephyr,mapped-partition nodes.

parameterdescription.para: DTS node label of a partition

simplesect.para: offset, as defined for the partition in DTS.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_OFFSET()
Get partition address from DTS node label.

parameterdescription.para: DTS node label of a partition

simplesect.para: address, as defined for the partition in DTS.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_ADDRESS()
Get partition address from DTS node.

simplesect.para: address, as defined for the partition in DTS.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_NODE_ADDRESS()

memberdef.name: PARTITION_NODE_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: COND_CASE_1(DT_NODE_HAS_COMPAT(node, zephyr_mapped_partition), \ (DT_MAPPED_PARTITION_OFFSET(node)), \ DT_FIXED_SUBPARTITION_EXISTS(node), \ (DT_PROP_BY_IDX(DT_PARENT(node), reg, 0) + DT_PROP_BY_IDX(node, reg, 0)), \ ((DT_PROP_BY_IDX(node, reg, 0))))

briefdescription.para: Get partition offset from DTS node.

detaileddescription.para: Note: This only works from a top level fixed-partitions node, top level fixed-subpartitions node or fixed-partitions node inside of 1 layer of a fixed-subpartitions node, it will not work for multiple layers of fixed-subpartitions nodes, though this works on all instances of zephyr,mapped-partition nodes.

simplesect.para: offset, as defined for the partition in DTS.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_NODE_OFFSET()
Get partition size for DTS node label.

simplesect.para: size, as defined for the partition in DTS.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_SIZE()
Get fixed-partition size for DTS node.

simplesect.para: size, as defined for the partition in DTS.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_NODE_SIZE()
Get device pointer for device the area/partition resides on.

parameterdescription.para: DTS node label of a partition

simplesect.para: const struct device type pointer

briefdescription.para: Get device pointer for device the area/partition resides on.

parameterdescription.para: DTS node label of a partition

simplesect.para: Pointer to a device.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_DEVICE()
Get device pointer for device the area/partition resides on.

simplesect.para: Pointer to a device.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_NODE_DEVICE()
Get the node identifier of the flash controller the area/partition resides on.

parameterdescription.para: DTS node label of a partition

simplesect.para: Pointer to a device.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_OFFSET()
Get the node identifier of the flash controller the area/partition resides on.

simplesect.para: Pointer to a device.

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_NODE_MTD()
Get pointer to flash_area object by partition label.

parameterdescription.para: DTS node label of a partition

simplesect.para: Pointer to flash_area type object representing partition

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION()
Get pointer to flash_area object by partition node in DTS.

simplesect.para: Pointer to flash_area type object representing partition

briefdescription.para: Deprecated macro, replace with PARTITION_OFFSET()
Abstraction over flash partitions/areas and their drivers.
